title: ACT I
subtitle: The Verdant Shard
rewardRelicDesc: Your base gains +10 max HP at the start of every battle.

intro.title: THE FRACTURE
intro.text:
Three years ago, the Grand Dominion shattered.

Not in war. Not gradually. In a single catastrophic instant — a magical detonation the scholars call the Fracture Event. The realm split into isolated shards, each one sealed behind walls of warped space and collapsed ley lines.

Nobody knows who caused it. Nobody knows how to undo it.

intro.title: THE WANDERER
intro.text:
You are Jarv. Former tactician of the Dominion's western campaigns. Now, technically, unemployed.

The army you served no longer exists. The city you lived in is cut off behind a shard wall. The people you knew are either dead, stranded, or figuring out their own problems.

You have a deck of cards, a vague sense of mission, and the navigational instincts of someone who has been lost before and considers it acceptable.

intro.title: THE VERDANT SHARD
intro.text:
Your compass points to the nearest reachable shard — a dense, ancient forest realm that has grown wild and hostile since the Fracture closed its borders.

The shard's guardian, a being called the Thornlord, has sealed its internal pathways. Local traders say he was old before the Dominion was founded.

Local traders also say he hasn't spoken to anyone in forty years. You're choosing to interpret that as optimistically as possible.

intro.title: YOUR MISSION
intro.text:
Break through the shard. Reach the Thornlord. Defeat him. Earn passage.

Take whatever cards and crystals you can carry out the other side — your collection grows with every run, your mastery deepens, and somewhere in the Fractured Core the answer to all of this is waiting.

Probably.

outro.title: THE THORNLORD FALLS
outro.text:
The ancient guardian crumples. Roots unravel. Bark splits along fracture lines older than the Dominion.

For a moment the whole forest holds its breath — then it exhales. The sealed pathways glow faintly and open like doors that have been waiting for exactly this.

outro.title: WHAT HE WAS
outro.text:
The Thornlord was not evil. He was old, and afraid, and doing what guardians do when the world stops making sense.

You take the Bark Shield he leaves behind. Not as a trophy. As a reminder that some things only break because nothing else would hold.

outro.title: THE ROAD AHEAD
outro.text:
One shard cleared. The ley lines pulse — faint, but connected.

Your deck returns to its bones. Your collection carries forward. Somewhere beyond the Verdant Shard, the Iron Citadel waits — and whoever controls it will not be glad to see you.

Good.

jarvMoods: Now, in the administrative sense, without portfolio.
Now — if you were being generous — on sabbatical.
jarvIntros: You have a deck of cards, a vague sense of mission, and the navigational instincts of someone who has been lost before and considers it acceptable.
You have a deck of cards, a half-remembered oath, and an extremely stubborn refusal to think too hard about any of this.
You have a deck of cards, a persistent headache, and the growing suspicion that this wasn't your first time here.
You have a deck of cards, a strategy you haven't fully thought through, and the particular confidence of someone with nothing left to lose.
You have a deck of cards. You always have a deck of cards. That, at least, has been consistent.
forestDesc: Your compass points to the nearest reachable shard — a dense, ancient forest realm that has grown wild and hostile since the Fracture closed its borders.
Your compass points to the Verdant Shard — a place that smells of pine resin and old magic, where the trees remember things the people forgot.
Your compass points toward the Verdant Shard. The forest ahead is old enough to have opinions about you.
The Verdant Shard pulses at the edge of your compass bearing — a realm of old growth and older grudges, sealed since the Fracture.
thornlordDesc:
The shard's guardian, a being called the Thornlord, has sealed its internal pathways. Local traders say he was old before the Dominion was founded.

Local traders also say he hasn't spoken to anyone in forty years. You're choosing to interpret that as optimistically as possible.

The Thornlord seals the shard's pathways. He is ancient, uncompromising, and deeply inconvenient. You've dealt with worse. Probably.

At least, it feels like you have.

The Thornlord waits at the end of the shard. Older than the Dominion. Older than most things people are afraid of. He has not spoken to a living soul in forty years.

You wonder, briefly, if he'll recognise you.

The Thornlord seals every path through the Verdant Shard. Traders who've survived say he is patient, thorough, and has never once reconsidered a decision.

You have a plan. You've had plans before.
priorRunSummaries: The path felt familiar — the way a dream feels familiar, just before it turns wrong.
You stepped into the Verdant Shard with the uneasy sense that you'd stepped here before. You pushed the feeling aside. There was work to do.
Something about the first battle. The angle of the light. The particular way the Goblins charged. A déjà vu so precise it was almost a memory.
The forest seemed quieter than it should have been. As though it recognised you, and was deciding whether to be pleased about it.
priorRunOpenings: You remember — and here is the strange part — getting this far before. Not the same choices. But the same road.
The scholar on the boulder didn't look up when you approached. 'Again?' he said. 'You're ahead of schedule.'
The Thornlord, when you finally stood before him, was already watching you. As though he had been expecting you. As though he had been expecting this for some time.
The goblins at the first barricade looked familiar. Not familiar the way people are familiar — familiar the way furniture in a childhood home is familiar. Something about the arrangement of them.
milestoneOpenings5: The fifth time through the Verdant Shard, a bird called out your name. That was new.
Fifth campaign. The Thornlord paused before his opening monologue this time. As though choosing different words.
milestoneOpenings10: Ten campaigns. The Wandering Scholar had written something in his margins. Your name. A question mark.
You notice things on the tenth pass that you hadn't noticed before. The way the Forest Patrol waits a little too perfectly. The way the shrine seems to already know your choice.
milestoneOpenings25: Twenty-five campaigns. The Thornlord opens with silence now, instead of threats. It feels more honest.
After twenty-five runs, the goblins at the first checkpoint have started waving before attacking you. You choose not to read into this.
milestoneOpenings50: Something is different this time. The sky above the Verdant Shard is slightly the wrong colour. The compass hesitates before pointing.
On the fiftieth campaign, you notice the cracks. Small ones. In the edges of things. The world is tired of resetting itself.
milestoneOpenings100:
The Thornlord looks past you. Past Jarv. Directly at the person holding the cards.

'You again,' he says. 'Not the tactician. You. I know you. I've been watching you since the beginning.'

A pause. A root curls against the ground like a finger drumming.

'A hundred times you've come here. A hundred times I've fallen. I am the oldest thing in this shard and you have made me your tutorial boss.'

He sounds almost amused. Almost.

midRunTemplates: The {ordinalLower} time through the Verdant Shard. The Thornlord has stopped rehearsing his opening speech.
Campaign {n}. The Wandering Scholar barely looks up anymore. He has your name pre-written in the margin.
{n} campaigns in. The goblins at the first barricade have started placing bets on how quickly you'll get past them.
{n} times you've stood at this shard's edge. The trees have started leaning away slightly when you approach.
Run {n}. You walk in before dawn, which is new. Everything else is exactly as you left it.
The {ordinalLower} attempt. You recognise the smell now. Damp bark and old magic and something else — familiarity.
{n} campaigns. The Wandering Scholar has started making tea before you arrive. He knows the timing by heart.

panels.title: YOUR MISSION
panels.text:
Break through. Reach the Thornlord. Defeat him.

You know the way. You always know the way.

panels.title: THE WANDERER

You know the way. You always know the way.

You know how this goes. You've always known how this goes.

goblin-raid.label: Goblin Raiders
goblin-raid.description: A rowdy mob has blockaded the trade route. Clear them out.

camp.label: Woodland Camp
camp.description: A sheltered clearing. Rest here and tend your wounds.

shrine.label: Ancient Shrine
shrine.description: A moss-covered shrine pulses with faint forest magic.

eventConfig.title: A Forgotten Shrine
eventConfig.description: Buried deep in moss and roots, an ancient shrine pulses with faint magic. Carved glyphs glow a dim green. Something still listens here.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The shrine accepts your gift — restored 12 HP

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The glow brightens — +15 crystals

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The shrine is silent. Nothing happens.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: Something drains your offering — lose 5 HP

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: Something small materialises at the altar's base — added to inventory

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The shrine blesses you with renewed fortune — +1 life

pools.label: Pray for strength
pools.consequence: A card materialises from the glow — uncommon

pools.label: Pray for strength
pools.consequence: The magic guides your wounds — restored 8 HP

pools.label: Pray for strength
pools.consequence: The shrine tests you — lose 10 HP

pools.label: Pray for strength
pools.consequence: You feel oddly lucky — +20 crystals

pools.label: Pray for strength
pools.consequence: Something small and inexplicable materialises in your palm — added to inventory

pools.label: Pray for strength
pools.consequence: A strange object appears in your hand from nowhere — added to inventory

pools.label: Pocket the ritual stones
pools.consequence: You grab them and run — +18 crystals, lose 8 HP

pools.label: Pocket the ritual stones
pools.consequence: A common card falls from the stones

pools.label: Pocket the ritual stones
pools.consequence: The stones crumble to dust. Nothing.

pools.label: Pocket the ritual stones
pools.consequence: The shrine curses you — lose 15 HP

pools.label: Pocket the ritual stones
pools.consequence: One of the stones turns out to be something stranger — added to inventory

forest-path.label: Forest Path
forest-path.description: A narrow trail — someone has been using it. Someone unfriendly.

patrol.label: Forest Patrol
patrol.description: A disciplined warband is watching the deeper path.

ambush.label: The Ambush
ambush.description: Something ancient was waiting in the treeline.

ruins.label: Watchtower Ruins
ruins.description: A crumbling garrison tower leans against the treeline.

eventConfig.title: Crumbling Watchtower
eventConfig.description: A moss-eaten garrison tower leans against the treeline, abandoned mid-battle. A rusted sword stands upright in the earth beside it. The armory gate hangs open.

pools.label: Rest in the shelter
pools.consequence: The quiet heals — restored 15 HP

pools.label: Rest in the shelter
pools.consequence: You sleep badly. Restored 8 HP

pools.label: Rest in the shelter
pools.consequence: Something bites you in the night — lose 6 HP

pools.label: Rest in the shelter
pools.consequence: You find a pouch under the floorboards — +14 crystals

pools.label: Rest in the shelter
pools.consequence: You wake to find a small curiosity beside you — added to inventory

pools.label: Rest in the shelter
pools.consequence: Something rolls under your hand in the dark — added to inventory

pools.label: Search the armory
pools.consequence: An uncommon card hidden behind a loose brick

pools.label: Search the armory
pools.consequence: A rare weapon contract — add a rare card

pools.label: Search the armory
pools.consequence: Old coin cache — +20 crystals

pools.label: Search the armory
pools.consequence: A rusted trap springs — lose 10 HP

pools.label: Search the armory
pools.consequence: You find a curious object among the debris — added to inventory

pools.label: Search the armory
pools.consequence: Something rolls out of the rubble — added to inventory

pools.label: Climb the watchtower
pools.consequence: Good vantage point — +12 crystals from spotted supply cache

pools.label: Climb the watchtower
pools.consequence: A common card is wedged in the battlements

pools.label: Climb the watchtower
pools.consequence: The stairs collapse — lose 12 HP

pools.label: Climb the watchtower
pools.consequence: Nothing up there. Wind. Old regrets.

pools.label: Climb the watchtower
pools.consequence: Something is jammed in the bell — you pocket it — added to inventory

pools.label: Climb the watchtower
pools.consequence: You spot a hidden passage to a safe camp below — +1 life

war-camp.label: War Camp
war-camp.description: A fortified enemy camp blocks the road ahead.

market.label: Travelling Market
market.description: A merchant cart half-hidden in the underbrush. She waves you over.

overgrowth.label: Overgrowth
overgrowth.description: The forest has reclaimed the road. The things living in it haven't welcomed travellers in a long time.

captain.label: Forest Captain
captain.description: A hardened veteran with well-drilled siege troops — and something the forest has been teaching him about patience.
bossDialogue: "You've come a long way for a card player."
"The Thornlord doesn't see visitors. That's why I'm here."

deep-woods.label: Deep Woods
deep-woods.description: Far enough from the patrol routes to be safe. The forest is quieter here. Almost welcoming.

troll-bridge.label: Troll Bridge
troll-bridge.description: Something large lives under the bridge. It has been living there since before the Fracture. It is not pleased about visitors.

old-shrine.label: Old Shrine
old-shrine.description: A second shrine, deeper in the forest — older than the first, and considerably less friendly about it.

eventConfig.title: The Elder Shrine
eventConfig.description: The second shrine sits darker and deeper than the first — the carvings worn to near illegibility, the offerings long rotted. Whatever power it holds, it has not faded. It has simply become unfamiliar with generosity.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The shrine absorbs it in silence. Restored 10 HP.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The glow shifts to amber. +18 crystals.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The shrine takes your offering. Nothing else happens.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: Something hungry stirs — lose 8 HP.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The shrine blesses you with renewed fortune — +1 life.

pools.label: Pray into the dark
pools.consequence: A card emerges from the glow — uncommon.

pools.label: Pray into the dark
pools.consequence: The shrine drains something from you — lose 12 HP.

pools.label: Pray into the dark
pools.consequence: A strange warmth answers. Restored 10 HP.

pools.label: Pray into the dark
pools.consequence: Coins appear at the base. +22 crystals.

pools.label: Pry a glyph-stone free
pools.consequence: You pull it loose and run — +20 crystals, lose 10 HP.

pools.label: Pry a glyph-stone free
pools.consequence: The stone crumbles to ash. Nothing.

pools.label: Pry a glyph-stone free
pools.consequence: A common card tumbles out with it.

pools.label: Pry a glyph-stone free
pools.consequence: The shrine curses you — lose 18 HP.

thornwall-pass.label: Thornwall Pass
thornwall-pass.description: The Thornlord's outer defences — living walls of thorn and root, older than any siege weapon.

thornlord.label: The Thornlord
thornlord.description: Ancient guardian of the Verdant Shard. Unbowed for centuries.
bossDialogue: "You carry the smell of the shattered world."
"I sealed these paths to keep the rot from spreading. Clearly I should have built higher walls."
"You will not pass. The Verdant Shard does not need another wandering tactician."

deep-crossing.label: Deep Crossing
deep-crossing.description: A collapsed stone bridge over a dark ravine. Something is defending it.
